validation.notes: Two delivery surfaces: Python TDD (steps 01-01 through 01-04) and markdown forge (step 02-01). All existing PES adapters reused read-only — no adapter changes required.

phases.name: Python PES — Domain, Service, Port, Adapter, CLI
phases.description: Domain model, snapshot service, port interface, filesystem adapter, CLI entry point

steps.name: Feedback domain model
criteria: FeedbackType enum has BUG, SUGGESTION, and QUALITY values
QualityRatings holds four optional int fields (1-5 each): past_performance, image_quality, writing_quality, topic_scoring
FeedbackSnapshot captures 14 context fields including plugin_version, proposal metadata, wave state, and artifact list
FeedbackEntry composes type, ratings, free_text, and snapshot; to_dict() produces JSON-serializable output
All dataclasses are frozen or immutable where state should not change after construction
steps.implementation_notes: delivery: code/tdd. Pure domain objects — no infrastructure imports. FeedbackEntry.to_dict() must round-trip through JSON without loss.

steps.name: FeedbackSnapshotService — pure context assembly
criteria: build_snapshot() accepts pre-read state, rigor, profile, finder dicts plus mtimes and cwd — no IO inside the method
Snapshot excludes capability text, past_performance descriptions, draft content, and key_personnel details
completed_waves and skipped_waves derived from waves dict by reading each entry's status field
top_scored_topics limited to 5 entries containing only topic_id, composite_score, and recommendation
plugin_version obtained via subprocess git rev-parse --short HEAD with 'unknown' fallback on non-zero exit
steps.implementation_notes: delivery: code/tdd. Pure function — all inputs passed as arguments. Privacy boundary enforced here: fields not in approved list are never added to snapshot.

steps.name: FeedbackWriterPort and FilesystemFeedbackAdapter
criteria: FeedbackWriterPort is an ABC with a single write(entry, output_dir) -> Path abstract method
FilesystemFeedbackAdapter implements FeedbackWriterPort with atomic write (.tmp -> .bak -> rename)
Feedback filename is feedback-{timestamp}.json with colons replaced by hyphens
Output directory is created if absent before writing
Adapter test verifies written file is valid JSON matching entry.to_dict() output
steps.implementation_notes: delivery: code/tdd. Atomic write follows project .tmp/.bak/rename protocol. Port in ports/, adapter in adapters/ — mirrors existing rigor_port / filesystem_rigor_adapter pattern.

steps.name: sbir_feedback_cli.py — save subcommand
criteria: save subcommand accepts --type, --ratings (JSON string), --free-text, --state-dir, --profile-path, --feedback-dir arguments
All adapter reads degrade gracefully to None on missing or unreadable files — no missing file blocks submission
Successful save prints JSON {feedback_id, file_path} to stdout and exits 0
Non-zero exit code with error message on unrecognized feedback type
CLI wires WorkspaceResolver, JsonStateAdapter, FilesystemRigorAdapter, JsonProfileAdapter, JsonFinderResultsAdapter without modifying any of them
steps.implementation_notes: delivery: code/tdd. Pattern matches dsip_cli.py (subcommand dispatch, argparse, JSON stdout). Acceptance tests in tests/acceptance/sbir_developer_feedback/ drive milestone-03-cli.feature and walking-skeleton.feature.

phases.name: Markdown Forge — Command and Agent
phases.description: Slash command entry point and interactive feedback-collector agent

steps.name: proposal-developer-feedback command and sbir-feedback-collector agent
criteria: Command name is /sbir:developer-feedback and dispatches to sbir-feedback-collector agent
Command requires no context files — works without any active proposal state
Agent AskUserQuestion flow: type first, then ratings (only when type is Quality), then optional free text
Agent calls sbir_feedback_cli.py save via Bash with correct arguments and parses JSON output
Agent confirms to user with feedback_id and file_path on success; reports error message on non-zero CLI exit
steps.implementation_notes: delivery: forge/command + forge/agent. No pytest tests. Validated via /nw:forge checklist. Agent size <= 400 lines. No skills required — all logic delegated to CLI.
